title: oporinia autumnalis var. alpina
intro: Oporinia autumnalis var. alpina, also known as Alpine Wood Sorrel, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Saxifragaceae family, characterized by its small, round leaves and white to pinkish flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its astringent and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high tannin content. The health benefits of Alpine Wood Sorrel include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and act as an antimicrobial agent, making it a popular choice in herbal remedies. Alpine Wood Sorrel can be prepared in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to address various health concerns and promote overall well-being.
